56.reason: Infinite loop: the condition uses p1 (the head node) which never changes; should use p (the traversal pointer) to iterate over terms of p1.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The loop condition uses p1 instead of p, causing an infinite loop because p1 never changes. Also, the loop should iterate over all terms of p1, but p is initialized to p1->link and never used correctly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: The loop condition uses p1 instead of p, causing an infinite loop because p1 never changes. Also, the loop should iterate over all terms of p1, but p is initialized to p1->link and never used correctly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  The loop condition uses p1, which never changes, causing an infinite loop. Should use p (or properly update p1).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Infinite loop: the condition uses p1 (the head node) which never changes; should use p (the traversal pointer) to iterate over terms of p1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Logical Error: The loop condition checks p1 instead of p. Since p1 is not updated inside the loop, this causes an infinite loop if p1 is not NULL, leading to Runtime Error (Memory Limit Exceeded or Timeout). It should be 'while(p!=NULL)']
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Logical Error: The loop condition checks p1 instead of p. Since p1 is not updated inside the loop, this causes an infinite loop if p1 is not NULL, leading to Runtime Error (Memory Exhaustion or Timeout). It should be 'while(p!=NULL)']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [The loop condition should be 'p != NULL' instead of 'p1 != NULL'. The variable 'p1' never changes in the loop, causing infinite loop or incorrect traversal. Also, 'p1' is a header node and its data is unused, so multiplication should iterate over 'p' which points to actual terms.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Infinite loop: p1 never advances; should use 'p' which is correctly initialized to p1->link]

96.reason: Error: poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition modifies the lists in place, causing memory leaks and incorrect results. Also, the result of poly_muti is not freed.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition modifies the lists in place, causing memory leaks and incorrect results. Also, the result of poly_muti is not freed.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition modifies the lists in place, causing memory leaks and incorrect results. Also, the result of poly_muti is not freed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition merges two lists destructively by linking nodes. Repeated calls will cause memory issues and incorrect merging since intermediate results are not properly handled. Also, poly_muti itself is broken as noted above.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition merges two sorted lists; however, the result of poly_muti is unsorted and not combined properly across multiple terms, leading to incorrect merging and memory issues]

61.reason: Error: p is incremented but never used to update p1, so p1 remains the same, causing infinite loop.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: p is incremented but never used to update p1, so p1 remains the same, causing infinite loop.

84.reason: [Incorrect input termination: mixing scanf with getchar may leave newline in buffer, causing premature exit or infinite loop; also, second polynomial input may not read correctly due to leftover '\n']

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This loop uses 'scanf(...) != EOF' but also checks for newline with getchar(). This can cause issues when input ends without trailing newline or when mixing scanf and getchar. For the given test case with only one line for second polynomial, this may consume extra input or fail to terminate correctly. However, the primary runtime error stems from poly_muti's logic.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect input termination: mixing scanf with getchar may leave newline in buffer, causing premature exit or infinite loop; also, second polynomial input may not read correctly due to leftover '\n']
